posts.body: Predictions: 9 of 9 Pending | Tied for 1st with 690 others The UFC has consistently been making the worst decisions possible as of late, in what world do you put a rematch of a fight that sold over 18 thousand tickets out in japan in the apex in front of no crowd.

posts.author: B7L7
posts.body: Predictions: 9 of 9 Pending | Tied for 1st with 690 others Last time they fought Kyoji showed levels. He was one step ahead and just faster in every single striking exchange. That said, he’s still fast but is 8 years older, and Kape has improved drastically. Kape is also so much bigger than he was back then, and is on that Ali Abdelaziz/Dagestani gear. It should be a different fight this time around. * Edited at 03.26.2026, 12:00 PM ET *

posts.author: battousai
posts.body: This rematch was made for a reason and it's because the UFC cannot stand Manel Kape and want him out of the title picture ASAP. Kyoji Horiguchi has been the uncrowned featherweight champion for going on a decade now. No doubt Kape is better compared to the first time they fought, but so is Horiguchi.Don't see this fight going any different than the first meeting and I think there's a very good chance the UFC is thinking the same.

posts.author: patrickmoore_1
posts.body: Predictions: 9 of 9 Pending | Tied for 1st with 690 others @battousai yeah im sure the ufc cant stand one of the most entertaining fighters in the ufc and one of the only fighters that actually tries to sell fights. According to kape the ufc said he could wait for a title shot but he chose this fight instead, kapes going to smoke kyogi

posts.author: Grogers70426
posts.body: Predictions: 8 of 9 Pending | Tied for 1st with 690 others @battousai when has Kyoji been considered the uncrowned featherweight champion considering he's been flyweight and sometimes bantamweight. I'm hoping that he schools Kape again. I still have memories of the Mokaev fight, considering they kept trying to fight in the hotel and everywhere else except for the cage. Well they were still aggressive until the commission and camera guys got out the cage. Granted, he did better than I expected against Royval, but I was expecting Royval to win

posts.author: battousai
posts.body: @Grogers70426 Kyoji has only lost once at flyweight ever and it was to Mighty Mouse. Thats why I say hes kind of the uncrowned king of that weight class right now. Hes technically on a 5 fight win streak in the UFC and hasnt lost overall in 3 years. Hes as legit a contender as anybody and if he beats Kape again he will be the #1 contender no questions asked. The only arguement I see for Kape is this rematch being at 125lbs somehow being an advantage, which Im not sure is sound but it's the only variable that's different. I say this as someone who acknowledges both guys are better than the fist time they fought. I just think Kyoji is still the more complete fighter. Its why he won the first time and its why I think he wins again.

posts.author: battousai
posts.body: @Spockling Patchy Mix is gigantic compared to Kyoji and is a respectable loss. Kyoji fought Patchy when he was at his peak, not the version everyone is watching struggle lately. Kyoji was also beating the breaks off of Sergio Pettis before he got caught in their first fight but then he got his revenge in the rematch.
